title: Kernel orthestrationについて、codexだけでなくglmやclaudeとの協奏として設定されているか確認せよ
summary_text: **Verdict:** the second-stage session canary is **necessary**, but only as a narrow guard-path check. Without it, startup smoke can still pass while real monitor/audit session resolution is broken. **Use this minimal contract (and stop there):** - Run canary only through the **supported launcher path** (`kernel` / `codex-prompt-launch`) in a PTY, never bare slash. - Inject a unique `CANARY_ID` and require a **new bootstrap evidence file** for that run with `claude.ok=true` and `glm.ok=true`. - Require launcher/guard exit `0` and explicitly fail on monitor messages like `startup quorum`, `could not resolve rollout session`, `monitor did not stop`, or `no rollout session created`. - Verify the resolved rollout session for this run reaches policy floor (active lanes + distinct families) via existing audit logic, not manifest text. - Keep bounded timing (for example startup timeout + small post-exit grace); fail closed on timeout. **What to avoid (overreach/flakiness):** - Long dwell “always-on” behavior in this canary. - Quota/rate-limit stress in this canary. - Any check that can pass from stale/global evidence windows. - Any path that allows single-lane fallback (treat as policy violation).
